allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps regression-to-the-mean distinguish ordinary reversion from a real structural change in the system.
allies.source_quote: Systems thinking teaches us that common system structures produce characteristic patterns of behavior.

allies.rationale_text: Probabilistic-thinking gives regression-to-the-mean the prior and base-rate discipline needed to treat extremes as less likely to persist.
allies.source_quote: Bayesian statistics (probabilistic thinking) provides the mathematical discipline to fight inherent biases and integrate new data with existing background knowledge (the prior probability or base rate).

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias makes regression-to-the-mean look false because observers prefer the treatment story to the reversion story.
antagonists.source_quote: confirmation bias will cause them to attribute the change to the reprimand, ignoring the RTM mechanism.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Regression-to-the-mean conflicts with representativeness-bias when extreme streaks are misread as persistent signal.
structured_tensions.source_quote: regression-to-the-mean vs representativeness-bias: regression-to-the-mean conflicts with representativeness-bias (representativeness bias) when extreme streaks are misread as persistent signal.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Regression-to-the-mean conflicts with survivorship-bias when only visible winners are used to infer stable exceptional performance.
structured_tensions.source_quote: regression-to-the-mean vs survivorship-bias: regression-to-the-mean conflicts with survivorship-bias (survivorship bias) when only visible winners are used to infer stable exceptional performance.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Regression-to-the-mean conflicts with confirmation-bias when observers attribute reversion to treatment effects because regression toward the mean contradicts the preferred causal story.
structured_tensions.source_quote: regression-to-the-mean vs confirmation-bias: regression-to-the-mean conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when observers attribute reversion to treatment effects because regression toward the mean contradicts the preferred causal story.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on explicit conflict blocks and on the alliance with statistical outside-view discipline, so the file stays close to that surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation expansion beyond the named variance-vs-story conflict set.
open_questions: Availability bias remains source-real as an antagonist, but the explicit tension block already captured the stronger persistent-signal conflicts.
